description_long: This is a modern hillside house complex featuring two main structures set on a sharply sloped terrain. The larger building has a gable roof, multiple large windows, and an expansive deck area, while the smaller building sits lower on the slope and is connected by a pathway. A rectangular swimming pool is situated between the two buildings, partially surrounded by a patio. The exterior walls appear to be finished in light-colored materials, likely wood or concrete, with dark roofing and trim. The property includes a flat parking or driveway area at the upper end of the slope, and the landscaping is minimal, with mostly grass and exposed earth. The overall design emphasizes clean lines, open views, and integration with the steep site.
description: A modern, multi-structure hillside house with gable roofs, large windows, a pool, and minimal landscaping on a steeply sloped lot.
description_view_0: Side view showing the steep slope, both buildings, the pool, and the upper parking area.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entryways, large windows, and the pool between the two buildings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the slope, both buildings, and the pool from a different angle.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the main building, the driveway or parking area, and the slope.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large picture windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a simple door with steps, minimal overhang, and no porch or columns, emphasizing clean modern lines.
